title: Aperture, Chamber, Blockage
description: As much as If I Had Legs I’d Kick You is about motherhood, and the specific difficulties Linda has with her daughter, I feel that it’s more broadly about the intense difficulties of caring for anything besides oneself.
hook: Motherhood and care examined through intimate film analysis—how vulnerability becomes the real subject.

title: Making of a Poem: Monzer Masri and Robyn Creswell on “A Palestinian, a Sudanese, and the third was a Moroccan”
description: “What I know is that I don’t want it to be poetic. The time has passed when my poetry was poetic—if in fact my poetry was ever poetic. My fate has always been to do something different.”
hook: Palestinian poet on rejecting 'poetic' language—political urgency reshaping what contemporary poetry can be.

title: Poetry That Brings a Gardener’s Perceptiveness to Encounters With the Body
description:
A couple weeks after I first met the poet Asa Drake, a package from Florida showed up in my mailbox. Inside were jars of sweet jam and pickled peppadews, which I immediately understood Asa had grown and preserved herself. To receive a package in the mail is to feel cherished in a particularly quaint and […]

The post Poetry That Brings a Gardener’s Perceptivenes
hook: Sensory precision in poetry: a gardener's eye turned inward, gift-giving as literary intimacy.

title: The Crime Lady: Mexico Dreaming
description:
Dear TCL Readers:

I have returned to New York City after one of the strangest travel days of my life, leaving Mexico just before a “shelter in place” order and arriving deep into a blizzard (I did not know that “driving across the GWB in a snowstorm” was a bucket-list item, but that’s now been checked off!) I hate cold but I do love the city right now, with so much snow blanketing everything even as the streets are ruthlessly salted and cleaned.
hook: Crime writer's Mexico travel essay—existential reflection woven through displacement and cultural observation.

title: How the Epstein Files Are Forcing a Reckoning with Power
description: Instead of providing closure, the release of thousands of documents has intensified the fear that the full truth may be unknowable—and that institutions cannot be relied on to provide it.
hook: Epstein documents reveal institutional failure and unknowability—how disclosure deepens rather than resolves trauma.
